market_summary: Iran draft peace deal dominates: Iranian state TV reports 14-point framework MOU with US would reopen Strait of Hormuz within 30 days, clear mines, lift port blockade. Oil crashed (WTI -6% to $88.3, Brent -4% to $96.28) on the news. Equities mixed — Dow +300pts to new ATH on lower oil/consumer staples, but Nasdaq lagged (QCOM -9.7%, NVDA -2.6%, AMD -2.8%). Gold fell ~1.5-2% to ~$4,400 as geopolitical premium continues unwinding. Silver slid to $74.50 (-$1.80). BTC held ~$75K, slight intraday gain. MSTR at $159.93 (-57% YoY from $457 high). MASSIVE data day tomorrow: GDP 2nd estimate, PCE, jobless claims, durable goods, personal income/spending all at 8:30 ET.
key_events_tomorrow: GDP Q1 2nd Estimate 8:30 ET — Advance was 2.0%, TradingEconomics showing 0.5% actual (massive miss if confirmed, stagflation signal)
Core PCE Price Index YoY 8:30 ET — Previous 3.3%, forecast 3.3%, TE showing 3.2% actual (slight cool)
Core PCE MoM 8:30 ET — Previous 0.3%, forecast 0.3%, TE showing 0.3% actual (in-line)
PCE Price Index YoY 8:30 ET — Previous 3.8%, forecast 3.8%, TE showing 3.5% actual (cooling)
PCE Price Index MoM 8:30 ET — Previous 0.5%, forecast 0.5%, TE showing 0.7% actual (HOT month)
Personal Income MoM 8:30 ET — Previous 0.4%, forecast 0.5%, TE showing 0.6% actual
Personal Spending MoM 8:30 ET — Previous 0.5%, forecast 0.6%, TE showing 0.9% actual (very strong)
Initial Jobless Claims 8:30 ET — Previous 211K, forecast 215K, TE showing 209K actual (strong labor)
Durable Goods Orders 8:30 ET — Previous 3.5%, forecast 3.4%, TE showing 0.8% actual (big miss)
New Home Sales 10:00 ET — Previous 670K, forecast 660K, TE showing 682K actual
Iran deal headline flow — remains primary risk driver for oil/metals
overnight_risks: Iran deal fragility — draft is unofficial, US has not confirmed terms. Breakdown = immediate oil spike + gold/silver safe-haven bid. Deal confirmation = further oil downside + metals pressure
GDP 0.5% vs 2.0% advance = stagflation shock if confirmed. Growth collapsing while PCE MoM runs hot (0.7% vs 0.5% forecast). Risk-off potential for Asia open
Japan wholesale inflation accelerating — BOJ tightening speculation adding pressure on Asian equities, stronger yen could support gold
BTC ETF outflow streak now 8 consecutive days (>$2B total). ETH ETFs 10 days of outflows ($216M/week). Dark pool IBIT trade (29.2M shares, $1.3B) triggered 1.5% BTC drop
China property sector instability + weak consumer demand persists — risk to global growth narrative
Personal spending 0.9% MoM vs 0.6% forecast — if confirmed, implies consumers front-running tariffs, not organic strength. Reversal risk in Q2

instrument_news.XAG-USD: Silver at $74.50, down $1.80. Iran deal premium unwind continues. BUT structural bull case intensifying: Silver Institute confirms 5th consecutive year of supply deficit. Solar PV demand projected 230M oz in 2026 (29% of industrial). AI data center silver demand (GPU thermal, connectors) expected to double by 2030. COT: non-commercial 34,591 long vs 9,920 short — specs trimming longs but not aggressively shorting. Support $72-73, resistance $78.80. Industrial demand floor holding despite geopolitical headwind.
instrument_news.XAU-USD: Gold ~$4,400, down 1.5-2%. Central bank buying surged to 243 tonnes Q1 2026 (+35% QoQ from 207t Q4 2025). BUT gold ETF outflows: $2.17B out vs $1.09B in last week — US and China led withdrawals ($635M and $623M respectively). COT: non-commercial 211K long vs 51K short, positioning not extreme. SSGA target $5,000. Iran deal + oil crash reducing inflation hedge demand near-term. Watch PCE data — if YoY cooling confirmed (3.5% vs 3.8%), real yields rise = gold headwind.
instrument_news.BTC-USD: BTC ~$75K, slight gain. CoinDesk reports BTC/gold 3-month uptrend has snapped. On-chain bullish: MVRV Z-Score 1.2 (far below cycle top 3.8), whale accumulation 270K BTC in 30 days (largest since 2013), exchange reserves at 7-year low (~3M BTC), long-term holders control 78.3% of supply (up from 74.1%). ETF outflows are bearish near-term (8-day streak, >$2B). Fear & Greed at 25 (extreme fear). Classic accumulation zone setup: smart money buying while retail exits.
instrument_news.ETH-USD: ETH ~$2,272-2,274. ETF outflows worse than BTC: 10 consecutive days, $216M/week. Following BTC lower with compressed ETH/BTC ratio. No independent catalyst. SEC delayed tokenized stock exemption ruling — removes a potential near-term catalyst. Needs BTC to lead any recovery.
instrument_news.MSTR: Strategy (MSTR) at $159.93, market cap $57.77B. Holdings now 843,738 BTC (up from 818,334 on May 3). Bought 24,869 BTC for $2.01B at avg $80,985 on May 17. Also retired $1.5B convertible debt at below-par. Average cost basis $75,700. At BTC $75K, they're roughly at cost basis — NAV discount widening. Funded via ATM equity + STRC preferred. -57.28% from 52-week high of $457.22.

metals_outlook: Near-term bearish pressure from Iran deal risk-off unwind and oil crash. Gold ETF outflows ($2.17B) outpacing inflows ($1.09B). However, structural supports are formidable: central bank buying 243t in Q1 (+35% QoQ), silver in 5th year of supply deficit, solar/AI industrial demand accelerating (230M oz from solar alone). COT positioning not extreme — no forced liquidation risk. If GDP truly printed 0.5% (vs 2.0% advance), stagflation narrative strengthens the medium-term metals bull case even as near-term Iran optimism weighs. Silver $120 thesis timeline extends if Iran deal holds but structural deficit thesis unaffected. Key watch: PCE data tomorrow — cooling YoY (3.5% vs 3.8%) with hot MoM (0.7%) = mixed signal for real yields.
oil_outlook: Oil crashed on Iran deal: WTI -6% to $88.3, Brent -4% to $96.28. Iran commits to clearing Hormuz mines, reopening shipping within 30 days of deal. Draft is 14-point framework, 60-day normalization period, no tolls, Iran manages strait with Oman. CRITICAL CAVEAT: deal is draft only, US has not confirmed. Oil-metals correlation: lower oil reduces inflation expectations, reducing gold's inflation-hedge appeal. But lower oil also reduces recession risk, supporting risk assets. If deal collapses: immediate reversal — WTI back above $94, gold/silver bid. OPEC+ would need to respond to any Hormuz reopening with supply management.
sentiment_shift: Macro regime shifting: (1) Iran deal creating massive geopolitical premium deflation across oil and metals, (2) GDP potentially collapsing to 0.5% while PCE MoM runs hot = textbook stagflation, (3) BTC on-chain metrics screaming accumulation zone (MVRV 1.2, whale buying 270K BTC, exchange reserves 7yr low) while ETF flows screaming distribution (8-day outflow streak), (4) Equities bifurcating — Dow ATH on consumer staples while tech sells off. This is a regime transition environment. Dominant narrative could flip overnight depending on Iran deal confirmation/collapse and PCE print interpretation. Position for volatility, not direction.
